% paper-ko-kci.tex — KCI-friendly Korean conference / journal paper template.
%
% NOTE: KCI does not provide a single canonical .cls file; each society
% (KIISE, KIPS, KIBME, ...) ships its own. This template targets a generic
% two-column "article" layout that satisfies KCI submission guidelines for
% double-blind / camera-ready PDFs. Replace the documentclass and
% \society* commands with a society-specific .cls when one is supplied.
% Note: KCI camera-ready is two-column, but Pandoc-emitted longtable cannot
% live in two-column mode. Use single column for the build; convert to
% two-column at submission time with the society's .cls.
\documentclass[10pt,a4paper]{article}

% --- Geometry: KCI-typical 2.5cm margins, 1cm gutter ---------------------
\usepackage[a4paper,margin=2.0cm,columnsep=0.7cm]{geometry}

% --- XeLaTeX + CJK --------------------------------------------------------
% NOTE: xeCJK omitted (not installed). Variable Noto Sans KR pinned by file
% to avoid fontspec auto-discovery breakage with the multi-axis TTF.
\usepackage{fontspec}
\setmainfont{Noto Sans KR}[Path=/home/jovyan/.fonts/, UprightFont=NotoSansKR-Regular.ttf]
\setsansfont{Noto Sans KR}[Path=/home/jovyan/.fonts/, UprightFont=NotoSansKR-Regular.ttf]
\setmonofont{$if(monofont)$$monofont$$else$DejaVu Sans Mono$endif$}
\usepackage{polyglossia}
\setdefaultlanguage{english}
\XeTeXlinebreaklocale "ko"
\XeTeXlinebreakskip = 0pt plus 1pt

% --- Typography ----------------------------------------------------------
\usepackage{microtype}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{caption}
% Pandoc-emitted environments and macros require these:
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{amssymb}
\usepackage{longtable}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{array}
\usepackage{calc}
\usepackage{etoolbox}
\usepackage{fancyvrb}
\usepackage{framed}
\providecommand{\tightlist}{\setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}}
\providecommand{\passthrough}[1]{#1}
\providecommand{\pandocbounded}[1]{#1}
\captionsetup{font=small,labelfont=bf,labelsep=period}
\usepackage{listings}
\lstset{
  basicstyle=\ttfamily\footnotesize,
  breaklines=true,
  columns=fullflexible,
  keepspaces=true,
  showstringspaces=false,
  frame=single,
  framerule=0.3pt,
}
\usepackage[dvipsnames]{xcolor}
\usepackage{abstract}
\renewcommand{\abstractnamefont}{\normalfont\bfseries}
\renewcommand{\abstracttextfont}{\normalfont\small\itshape}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\hypersetup{colorlinks=true,linkcolor=NavyBlue,urlcolor=NavyBlue,citecolor=NavyBlue}

% --- Bibliography (ACM/IEEE-compatible numeric style) --------------------
\usepackage[numbers,sort&compress]{natbib}
% IEEEtran.bst not installed; fall back to plainnat which ships with natbib.
\bibliographystyle{plainnat}

% --- KCI-style title block placeholders ----------------------------------
% TODO(placeholder): replace with the society-specific commands when known.
\newcommand{\kcitypeKO}{원저}                    % 논문 종별 (원저/단신/논평/...)
\newcommand{\kcitypeEN}{Original Article}

% Pandoc populates these via metadata. Default placeholders below.
\title{$title$}
\author{
  $for(author)$$author$$sep$ \and $endfor$ \\
  \small \textit{TODO: 소속 / Affiliation} \\
  \small \texttt{TODO: corresponding@example.org}
}
\date{$date$}

% Header-includes hook (used by Pandoc).
$for(header-includes)$
$header-includes$
$endfor$

\begin{document}

\maketitle
\begin{abstract}
  \noindent\textbf{초록.} $if(abstract-ko)$$abstract-ko$$else$$abstract$$endif$

  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{Abstract.} $if(abstract-en)$$abstract-en$$else$TODO: English abstract.$endif$

  \medskip
  \noindent\textbf{주제어 / Keywords:}
  $if(keywords)$$for(keywords)$$keywords$$sep$, $endfor$$endif$
\end{abstract}
\vspace{1em}

$body$

% --- References ----------------------------------------------------------
$if(bibliography)$
\bibliography{$for(bibliography)$$bibliography$$sep$,$endfor$}
$else$
% TODO(placeholder): point to docs/paper-ko/references.bib once authored.
% \bibliography{docs/paper-ko/references}
$endif$

\end{document}
